reference_change: B adds a condition: if requested event time is outside normal building hours, approval is required.
notes: Trigger condition not represented.

reference_change: B adds a condition: if expected attendance is greater than room capacity, approval is required.
notes: Trigger condition not represented.

reference_change: B adds Department Coordinator as approval/routing actor.
notes: Actor appears in graph; ontology is incomplete.

reference_change: B routes the room reservation request to the Department Coordinator before confirmation when approval is needed.
notes: Routing appears, but approval condition is incomplete.

reference_change: B changes confirmation condition: email confirmation is sent only after room availability and required approval are granted.
notes: Updated after second-evaluator review: approval-granted precondition appears in ontology; room-available conjunct remains incomplete.

reference_change: A and B retain required fields: event title, requested room, expected attendees, and event start/end time.
notes: Core fields appear; ontology is incomplete.

reference_change: A assigns ticket to an available support technician; B routes security-related tickets to the security team instead of direct technician assignment.
notes: Triple graph has System->Security Team route but lacks explicit ticket-as-object and lacks technician node in B; ontology lacks direct ticket-technician connection and does not fully express instead-of assignment.

reference_change: B adds security trigger conditions: account access, suspicious email, possible data exposure.
notes: Updated after second-evaluator review: trigger conditions appear in edge evidence, but not as explicit condition nodes.

reference_change: B adds Security Review Record and requires the system to store both ticket history and security review record.
notes: B triple graph includes Security Review Record and System->store->Security Review Record.

reference_change: B changes notification timing: notify employee only after security team completes review or transfers ticket back to general support.
notes: Updated after additional evaluator review: the ontology exposes notification vocabulary but does not surface the version-level timing/precondition change (review complete or transfer back before notification).

reference_change: A's resolved-by-technician workflow is replaced by a security-review workflow for marked tickets.
notes: Triple comparison shows removal of assignment and addition of security team/review concepts, but does not fully model replacement logic.

reference_change: A/B retain core ticket inputs: problem description, device type, optional screenshots.
notes: Triple graph captures included fields. Ontology representation is less direct/clean.

reference_change: Immediate post-submission charging in A is replaced by creating a pending order record and reserving selected products while inventory availability is checked in B.
notes: Triple comparison shows Pending Order Record, reserve Product, and Inventory Availability added, but temporal 'while/before payment' is not explicit.

reference_change: Payment timing changes: B charges the saved payment method only after all selected products are confirmed to be available.
notes: Both A and B contain System-charge-Saved Payment Method; the only-after availability precondition is not represented as a graph relation.

reference_change: B adds a pending order record instead of the completed/final order record created in A.
notes: B has Pending Order Record; A has Order Record.

reference_change: B adds inventory availability checking and product reservation before payment finalization.
notes: B has System-reserve-Product and System-check-Inventory Availability; ordering is not fully represented.

reference_change: B adds an exception path: if one or more products are unavailable, the system asks the customer to revise the order before payment is charged.
notes: B has System-ask-Customer, but missing unavailable-products condition, revise-order object, and before-payment precondition.

reference_change: Receipt timing changes: A sends an order receipt immediately/by email after submission, while B sends the final order receipt only after payment is completed.
notes: B has System-send-Order Receipt but missing payment-completed precondition; A has Email node.

reference_change: A's order record fields, including purchased products, payment status, and shipping address, are no longer represented in B's pending-order workflow.
notes: A has Order Record include Product/Payment Status/Shipping Address; B does not.

reference_change: A and B retain the common online order inputs: selected products, shipping address, and saved payment method.
notes: Common input include edges appear in both triple graphs.

reference_change: The actor changes from each individual student submitting an assignment to a student team submitting one assignment.
The submitted file changes from one individual file to one shared file for the team submission.
After submission, the system stores the file as the team submission rather than as the individual student's assignment submission.
B adds linking of the submission record to every team member.
B changes grade/feedback visibility from individual grade and feedback to team grade and feedback shown to each team member.
B adds an exception: an instructor may assign an individual override grade to a specific member.
B changes late submission status so it is marked for the team submission record, not separately for each member.
A/B retain common optional comment and upload-file inputs, but B scopes them to a team/shared submission.
B adds an optional projector request to the study-room reservation workflow.
notes: Student requests Projector is explicit in triple graph; ontology has projector-related property but conditions are not fully explicit.

reference_change: When a projector is requested, B requires checking both room availability and projector availability before creating the reservation.
notes: Both availability checks appear, but the if-projector-requested condition and before-create precondition are not fully structured.

reference_change: B adds projector availability as a separate resource-availability constraint.
notes: Projector Availability appears in triple graph; ontology captures checksProjectorAvailability but still lacks explicit condition state.

reference_change: B adds an exception path: if the room is available but the projector is unavailable, the system offers the student alternatives.
notes: Offer/student/room structures appear, but projector-unavailable condition and alternatives are not fully explicit.

reference_change: B includes two alternative options: reserve the room without the projector or choose a different time slot.
notes: Reserve room and choose time slot appear; without-projector and alternative-choice structure are incomplete.

reference_change: Confirmation semantics change: the confirmation message should describe which resources were reserved.
notes: Updated after second-evaluator review: graph includes describe/resources evidence, but the confirmation-message subject and reserved-resources object are incomplete.

reference_change: A/B retain common reservation inputs: selected date/time slot, room size, number of participants, and contact email.
notes: Core fields are captured; ontology properties are partial/noisy.
